subject: traffic
post: love how my commute turns into a full meditation session because the car in front hasn't moved in ten minutes. truly peak urban life experience.

subject: C/o Kancharapalem
post: Finally watched C/o Kancharapalem and honestly, it’s just pure heart. No big stars, no forced drama, just people living their lives in a way that feels so real. Wish more mainstream movies took notes on how to write simple, honest characters.

subject: online grocery delivery
post: nothing humbles you faster than realizing you ordered five packets of snacks but forgot to buy actual food for the week.

subject: Thithi (Kannada film)
post: still thinking about Thithi. it’s rare to find a movie that’s so funny and cynical at the same time without trying too hard. the way the entire village acts like it’s a documentary is just wild.

subject: family group chats
post: the 4 AM 'good morning' flower picture in the family group chat is a bold choice for someone who hasn't spoken to me since Diwali.

subject: Aavesham movie
post: Finally watched Aavesham and honestly Fahadh Faasil just carries the whole thing on his back. That shift in character midway through caught me so off guard, absolute madness.

subject: AI coding tools debugging
post: spent an hour fighting with an AI suggestion that looked perfect but had the most subtle logic error imaginable. sometimes it saves me half a day, other times it just gaslights me into thinking my own code is broken. ugh.

subject: procrastination
post: tell myself i’ll start this task in exactly ten minutes, then look up and realize two hours of random wikipedia rabbit holes have vanished. at this point i’m just gaslighting my own productivity.

subject: daytime mood shift
post: the 4pm crash is hitting different today, bas chai mil jaye toh thoda insaan jaisa feel karu

subject: AR Rahman and ARR songs
post: nothing hits quite like late 90s AR Rahman at 2 AM. just put on a random playlist and ended up listening to the Roja and Dil Se albums on loop. sach mein, the production on these tracks is still lightyears ahead of anything coming out today.

subject: gym motivation
post: my gym motivation usually lasts exactly until the moment i see a cloud outside and decide that walking to the car is too much of a workout for one day.

subject: sleep schedule struggle
post: why is my brain suddenly so active at 2am when i have to be up in four hours. just shut up and let me sleep yaar

subject: autocorrect
post: my phone really thinks i am constantly trying to text people about 'ducking' things. trust me, i have much more creative ways to be frustrated in real life.
